# Provide dual CJS + ESM builds via Babel

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The library was originally CJS-only. Modern bundlers (Webpack, Rollup) benefit from ESM for tree-shaking and static analysis. The PR title reads: "build(esm): provide esm version for bundlers" (commit `a40cabb`, 2018-02-07).

## Decision

Use Babel with two environment configurations (`.babelrc`) to produce dual builds:
- `BABEL_ENV=cjs` targets Node >= 4.7.2 with CommonJS output to `dist/cjs/`
- `BABEL_ENV=esm` targets last 2 browser versions with ES modules output to `dist/esm/`

The source (`index.js`) is authored using ESM `import`/`export` syntax.

Package.json exposes both via:
- `"main": "./dist/cjs/index.js"` for Node/CommonJS consumers
- `"module": "./dist/esm/index.js"` for bundlers

## Consequences

- Bundler consumers get tree-shakeable ESM
- Node consumers get CJS without requiring ESM support
- Source uses modern syntax; Babel handles backward compatibility
- Build step is required before publishing (enforced in CI)
